layer3_inference: SCADA may be running background diagnostics or preparing failover sequences internally
rejection_reason: Layer 3 already self-rejected this inference as SPECULATIVE (3+ assumptions, classification applied correctly). Layer 4 confirms the rejection. Three corrections ledger entries (CL-001, CL-004, CL-010) have previously stripped this identical inference class. No data supports any claim about SCADA internal process state.

layer3_inference: Grid operators may be performing off-log readiness activities, drills, or coordination
rejection_reason: Layer 3 already self-rejected as SPECULATIVE. Layer 4 confirms. Seven corrections ledger entries (CL-002, CL-003, CL-005, CL-007, CL-008, CL-009, CL-010) have rejected this inference class — the most thoroughly vetted and consistently rejected inference in the entire analysis chain. No data supports any claim about operator off-log activities.

layer3_inference: Fire Department may be pre-coordinating with water system operators or positioning for demand
rejection_reason: Layer 3 already self-rejected as SPECULATIVE. Layer 4 confirms. Per CL-005: no evidence of FD-operator coordination. FD demand is structurally unpredictable. Absence of demand is TRUE_NEGATIVE, not evidence of preparation.

layer3_inference: Calm period complacency feedback loop — readiness degradation during quiescent period
rejection_reason: Layer 3 correctly identified this loop as 'unscoreable' and did not attempt to score it. Layer 4 confirms the strip. Measuring complacency requires psychological state data that no SCADA system provides. Per LZ-EPH-003, this inference requires 3+ unproven assumptions (operators are complacent, complacency degrades response time, degraded response time would matter given automated-first architecture). SPECULATIVE by definition.
